title: Show HN: TormentNexus – Open-source AI control plane with 26K+ MCP tools
body:
{
  "title": "Show HN: TormentNexus – Open-source AI control plane with 26K+ MCP servers",
  "body": "Hey HN,

I’ve been building TormentNexus, an open-source control plane for AI agents that solves two pain points I kept hitting: (1) finding and orchestrating the right tools among a growing ecosystem of MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers, and (2) giving agents persistent memory without shipping data to third parties.

**What it does:**
- **Searchable catalog of 26,000+ MCP servers** – automatically indexed, with metadata and health checks. You can filter by domain (e.g., database, file system, API wrappers) and version.
- **Persistent memory system** – agents can store and retrieve structured memories (facts, preferences, conversation state) across sessions. Built on SQLite under the hood, so no external databases required.
- **Local LLM support** – runs with Ollama, llama.cpp, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. All data stays on your machine if you use local models.
- **Unified dashboard** – web UI to manage agents, browse the catalog, inspect memory logs, and view request/response traces.
- **Go backend, single binary** – compiles to a ~15MB static binary with no runtime dependencies. Cold start in under 200ms on a $5 VPS.

**Architecture (quick overview):**
- Go HTTP server using chi router, with WebSocket for real-time agent updates
- Catalog: periodically scrapes public MCP registries (GitHub, npm, PyPI) and stores metadata in a BoltDB bucket with full-text search via bleve
- Memory: each agent gets a dedicated SQLite file (WAL mode) with a schema for key-value memories + vector embeddings (via local onnx runtime)
- Agent runner: spawns goroutines per agent, each with its own context and MCP client pool; supports tool chaining and retry logic
- Dashboard: React SPA served from the binary, communicates via REST + SSE

**Why open source?** I think the MCP ecosystem needs an open, auditable hub—not a walled garden. The entire codebase is MIT-licensed.

**Links:**
- GitHub
